positional_bias.note: Positions re-derived from quiz.gift match sidecar correct_position_distribution exactly. A/B/C/D = 3/3/3/3 across 12 MCQ.

length_bias.note: No MCQ has correct-answer length > 1.20x distractor mean (feedback stripped). Closest ratios ~1.07-1.13 (q5, q9, q12), all under threshold. Correct answer is shortest in q11.

distractor_plausibility.note: All 36 distractors map to genuine beginner misconceptions (math =, comparison, subtraction, camelCase/PascalCase, case-insensitivity, int/float confusion, type() converting or rounding, keyword/version myths). No score-1 absurd distractors.

objective_coverage.note: All 5 corpus LOs covered: assign/reassign (q1,q2,q3); naming rules + snake_case (q4,q5,q6,q7,q13); four types int/float/str/bool (q8,q9); type() (q10,q11); dynamic typing (q12,q14). Every objective_ref matches a corpus LO.

scope_grounding.note: No forward references tested (no operators, truthiness, type conversion, f-strings, type hints, or match-case as concepts). q2's 'score + 50' reuses corpus §5.2 reassignment example, not an operator lesson. bool appears in q8 only as one of the four type names. Every question traceable to corpus §5.1-§5.8.

verdict: Pass — positional (3/3/3/3, max_share 0.25), length (0.0 longest-correct rate), plausibility (0.0 absurd), and coverage (5/5 LOs, no orphans) all clean; no scope violations; sidecar matches .gift.
